scenario: Iran conflict with Hormuz closure and decapitation strikes

historicalCase.name: Tanker War (Iran-Iraq)
historicalCase.summary: Iran and Iraq attacked shipping to deprive each other of oil revenue. U.S. escort operations eventually broke Iranian blockade strategy.

keyActors.name: Iran
objectives: Punish Gulf Arab backers of Iraq

strategicDynamics.factor: Hormuz closure unsustainable against great power
strategicDynamics.description: Iran could harass but not close strait against U.S. naval power
Iraq with Gulf backing outlasted isolated Iran

strategicDynamics.factor: Asymmetric warfare prolonged but did not win
strategicDynamics.description: Iranian innovations delayed defeat but not prevented
Reflagging operation made attacks politically costly

outcome.economicImpact: Insurance rates soared, oil prices volatile but supplies maintained
outcome.geopoliticalShift: U.S. establishes permanent Gulf naval presence; Iran isolated

lessons.insight: Hormuz can be harassed but not closed against determined great power
Mine warfare effective for denial, not control
Attrition warfare favors the economically integrated
caveats: Revolutionary regimes may endure more pain

parallels.makaveliNote: Weak but instructive parallel. Consider Hormuz closure unsustainable against great power with caution.

historicalCase.name: Suez Crisis
historicalCase.summary: Anglo-French-Israeli attempt to seize Suez Canal and topple Nasser. Successful military operation failed strategically due to superpower pressure and financial warfare.

strategicDynamics.description: Nasser survived, regime consolidated around anti-colonial resistance
U.S. threat to dump Sterling forced British withdrawal
Canal seized but operation politically untenable
Soviet nuclear threats + U.S. financial pressure ended conflict

outcome.economicImpact: British/French humiliation, end of imperial era
outcome.geopoliticalShift: U.S. replaces UK/France as Middle East arbiter; Soviet influence expands

lessons.insight: Decapitation strikes without follow-through invite prolonged resistance
caveats: Requires total military occupation to succeed

lessons.insight: Financial leverage can defeat military superiority
caveats: Requires control of reserve currency or key markets

lessons.insight: Secondary powers cannot challenge superpower consensus
caveats: Superpowers must agree on red lines

parallels.makaveliNote: Weak but instructive parallel. Consider Decapitation failed to collapse regime with caution.
